resources.string: The crown breaks the neck; the ruler smiles and bears its weight.
CROWN BREAKS THE NECK. THE RULER KEEPS SMILING.

The hall answers with ten thousand throats. Subjects and enemies kneel in identical rows, and the throne rises from rival skulls, star-metal, and praise. Every command returns as law before the echo dies. Reality waits outside, torn and pleading, but why serve it when the dream is flawless? The crown is heavy because humility was the price, and every price was paid gladly.
TEN THOUSAND CHEER. ENEMIES KNEEL. THE THRONE RISES FROM SKULLS AND STARS. EVERY COMMAND BECOMES LAW. REALITY WAITS OUTSIDE.
